what_it_does: Defines a 24 by 24 SVG icon showing a stacked image/photo motif using stroked vector shapes.
observations: Uses a 24 24 viewBox with explicit width and height of 24.
Sets fill to none and uses currentColor for stroke so the icon inherits text color.
Draws a front rounded rectangle, a small circular image detail, a landscape-like polyline, and a rear frame outline.
Uses stroke width 1.5 with round caps and joins.
standouts: Layered frame construction communicates an image stack.
All visible content is stroke-based with no filled shapes.
The icon is self-contained in a single compact SVG element.
conventions: Follows common inline icon conventions: 24px canvas, currentColor stroke, no fill, round line caps and joins.
Uses simple SVG primitives and paths without ids, classes, scripts, or metadata.
notes: A compact stroke icon for representing multiple images or a stacked image asset.
questions: Should the rear frame path intentionally remain open at the lower-right edge?
Is the 1.5 stroke width the intended standard for this icon set?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename suggests an image-stack icon, and the SVG content depicts stacked image frames, so the apparent purpose matches the content.
intention_signals: It seems intended to provide a reusable, theme-colored UI icon for image collections, galleries, or stacked media assets.
novelty: The rear frame is represented with a sparse open path rather than a full second rectangle.
contribution: Contributes a reusable stroked image-stack glyph with inherited color, compact dimensions, and clear layered-media semantics.
summary_for_embedding: This is a 24px currentColor SVG outline icon depicting stacked image frames with a small photo detail and landscape line.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for representing an image stack or gallery-like media collection in a design system icon set.
